pg6-p1:
passed, and confidence began to be replaced by cock-sureness. I could laugh at the gin mills. Now I had what it takes! One day I walked into a cafe to telephone. In no time I was beating on the bar asking myself how it happened. As the
whisky rose to my head I told myself I would manage better next time, but I might as well get good and drunk then. And I
did.
pg6-p2-s1: The remorse, horror and hopelessness of the next morning are unforgettable.
pg6-p2-s2: The courage to do battle was not there.
pg6-p2-s3:
My
brain raced uncontrollably and there was a terrible sense of impending calamity.
pg6-p2-s4:
I hardly dared cross the street, lest I
collapse and be run down by an early morning truck, for it was scarcely daylight.
pg6-p2-s5:
An all night place supplied me with a
dozen glasses of ale.
pg6-p2-s6: My writhing nerves were stilled at last.
pg6-p2-s7:
A morning paper told me the market had gone to hell
again.
pg6-p2-s9: The market would recover, but I wouldn't.
pg6-p2-s13: Then a mental fog settled down.
pg6-p2:
The remorse, horror and hopelessness of the next morning are unforgettable. The courage to do battle was not there. My
brain raced uncontrollably and there was a terrible sense of impending calamity. I hardly dared cross the street, lest I
collapse and be run down by an early morning truck, for it was scarcely daylight. An all night place supplied me with a
dozen glasses of ale. My writhing nerves were stilled at last. A morning paper told me the market had gone to hell
again. Well, so had I. The market would recover, but I wouldn't. That was a hard thought. Should I kill myself? No - not
now. Then a mental fog settled down. Gin would fix that. So two bottles, and - oblivion.
pg6-p3-s1: The mind and body are marvelous mechanisms, for mine endured this agony two more years.
pg6-p3-s2:
Sometimes I stole from my wife's
slender purse when the morning terror and madness were on me.
pg6-p3-s3:
Again I swayed dizzily before an open window, or the
medicine cabinet where there was poison, cursing myself for a weakling.
pg6-p3-s4:
There were flights from city to country and
back, as my wife and I sought escape.
pg6-p3-s5:
Then came the night when the physical and mental torture was so hellish I feared I
would burst through my window, sash and all.
pg6-p3-s6:
Somehow I managed to drag my mattress to a lower floor, lest I suddenly
leap.
pg6-p3:
The mind and body are marvelous mechanisms, for mine endured this agony two more years. Sometimes I stole from my wife's
slender purse when the morning terror and madness were on me. Again I swayed dizzily before an open window, or the
medicine cabinet where there was poison, cursing myself for a weakling. There were flights from city to country and
back, as my wife and I sought escape. Then came the night when the physical and mental torture was so hellish I feared I
would burst through my window, sash and all. Somehow I managed to drag my mattress to a lower floor, lest I suddenly
